text:
Once upon a time in a tiny village nestled between mountains, there lived a beautiful girl named Olive. She loved nothing more than climbing trees. One sunny day, Olive discovered an old oak tree with hollows big enough to climb.

She climbed upward, losing herself in the whispers of ancient leaves. Suddenly, a giant owl flew down. It had large feathers and eyes as green as ovals. She didnaxed.

"You've decided to become featherweight?"

Olive giggled.

"You've got wings. You should fly."

So the wise owl taught Olive ancient fly techniques. Together they soared across cloud fields. Birds came to watch. Stars winked at them. Hours passed.

At dusk, Olive tired.

"Did you enjoy your flight?"

"A little."

That night, a gentle rain fell. Olive slept snugly inside the owl's big chest feathers.

Morning came, and Olive flew back.

The villagers hailed her.

"You saved us again!"

Just nod. You know birds.
